item.title: Thai Hospitality Group Minor International Said to Mull Singapore IPO Instead of HK
item.description: Thailand’s Minor International Pcl is considering listing its restaurant business in Singapore instead of Hong Kong as it sees greater interest from potential investors in the city-state, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: US-Backed Philippines AI Hub Could Draw at Least $10 Billion in Investments
item.description: A US-backed plan to build an artificial intelligence hub in the Philippines could draw an initial investment of about $10 billion, an official said, and help prop up an economy battered by the Iran war.

item.title: Saudi IPO Delay Is Another Setback for the Lagging Stock Market
item.description: A postponed initial public offering in Saudi Arabia is the latest setback for a stock market that’s trailing global peers for the fourth year in a row.

item.title: SK Hynix ETF Boom Is Becoming a Force in the Stock’s Trading
item.description: One of the hottest artificial-intelligence stocks in South Korea is increasingly being driven by demand for products built on top of it.

item.title: South Africa Posts Largest Current-Account Surplus Since 2022
item.description: South Africa posted its largest current-account surplus in four years in the first quarter as the value of gold exports surged and imports dropped.

item.title: Why Indonesia’s Rupiah Keeps Hitting Record Lows
item.description: Indonesia’s rupiah — Asia’s worst-performing currency this year — has been under pressure since late 2024 when investors began reassessing the country’s fiscal outlook following the election of President Prabowo Subianto. Before this current period of volatility, the currency hadn’t traded at such weak levels since the Asian Financial Crisis of 1997-98.

item.title: How Houthis’ Red Sea Shipping Threat Risks Bigger Oil Shock
item.description: One thing that’s helped limit the Iran war’s disruption to global oil supply is the ability of Saudi Arabia to use an alternative export route to the Strait of Hormuz: the Red Sea.

item.title: Blackstone to Back Two Sigma Veteran’s Quant Hedge Fund Startup
item.description: Jinger Zhao, a former Two Sigma Investments managing director who spent 18 years at the asset manager, is preparing to start her own Asia-focused hedge fund with initial backing from Blackstone Inc.

item.title: Norway Survey Dampens Chances of June Rate Hike as Economy Cools
item.description: Norway’s businesses presented a less rosy outlook in a key survey of corporate sentiment, likely easing pressure on the nation’s central bank to extend its interest-rate hikes next week.

item.title: US Stock Futures Bounce After Market Pullback; Oracle Declines
item.description: US equity futures advanced, bouncing after Wednesday’s late selloff in tech shares, while a swift end to renewed American strikes on Iran kept oil prices in check.

item.title: US, Turkey Resolve Key Issue on State Bank Ahead of Trump Visit
item.description: The US moved to resolve a key legal dispute with Turkey involving a state bank, alleviating a major irritant in bilateral ties ahead of President Donald Trump’s anticipated visit next month.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni Says EU Must Find Envoy to Speak to Russia
item.description: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni called on the European Union to appoint a representative to negotiate peace with Russia, inserting her voice into the debate over about how to engage with Vladimir Putin.

item.title: Moody’s Cuts UK Budget Hotel Chain Travelodge Deeper Into Junk
item.description: Moody’s Ratings slashed Travelodge Hotels Ltd’s credit rating deeper into junk, citing the company’s weaker financial performance and deteriorating debt metrics.

item.title: Why Tomatoes Are the Most Expensive They’ve Been in Four Decades
item.description: Fresh produce prices are getting more volatile.

item.title: Odd Lots: Why Tomato Prices are Getting More Volatile (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: Why Tomato Prices are Getting More Volatile (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: Why Tomato Prices are Getting More Volatile (Podcast)】

item.title: Italian Unicorn Satispay Plans €120 Million Funding Round
item.description: Satispay is planning to raise as much as €120 million ($139 million) to fund the Italian fintech’s expansion into new financial products and give it the flexibility to pursue acquisitions.

item.title: Meta Starts Unwinding Manus Deal by Splitting Operations, Data
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. has completed an operational split from Manus and halted data sharing between the two companies, taking a pivotal step toward unwinding a $2 billion acquisition opposed by Beijing.

item.title: Gilt Traders Brace for UK Vote With Burnham Eyeing Starmer’s Job
item.description: Bond investors including BNP Paribas Asset Management, Neuberger Berman and Allspring Global Investments are predicting a vote in a small northern constituency will ignite another bout of volatility in the UK’s $3 trillion debt market.

item.title: China Firm Secures Kenya Airport Revamp Deal After Adani Fallout
item.description: Kenya picked a Chinese firm for a $2.9 billion upgrade of its biggest airport, people with knowledge of the matter said, two years after a concession deal with Indian conglomerate Adani Group was canceled.

item.title: Ethiopia Plans Wider Budget Gap as Spending Jumps 21%
item.description: Ethiopia’s budget deficit is projected to widen to 1.4% of gross domestic product in the coming fiscal year.

item.title: Indian Banks Look to Foreign Funding to Maximize RBI’s Bazooka
item.description: Some Indian lenders have asked the central bank if they can help individuals access loans from overseas banks to bring in more capital under a new plan, according to people familiar with the matter, potentially amplifying inflows into the country.

item.title: Ryanair Probed Over Charging Parents to Sit With Children
item.description: Ryanair Holdings Plc. is facing a consumer investigation over fees that parents have to pay to sit next to their children.

item.title: Zimbabwe Seeking $150 Million AfDB Loan to Tackle Debt Default
item.description: Zimbabwe is in talks with the African Development Bank for a $150 million loan as part of efforts to clear billions of dollars of debt arrears, Finance Minister Mthuli Ncube said.

item.title: Audit Firms of Collapsed Lender MFS Probed by UK Watchdog
item.description: Accountancy firms linked to the collapsed mortgage lender Market Financial Solutions Ltd. are being investigated by the UK’s audit watchdog.

item.title: Kuwait Ships Cooking Gas Out of Hormuz as Gulf Producers Go Dark
item.description: Kuwait has shipped a cargo of liquefied petroleum gas out of the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz using a tanker it controls, as more producers opt for clandestine tactics to get energy to markets.

item.title: AI Wave Sparks Alarm in China With Call to Protect Worker Rights
item.description: China’s rapid adoption of artificial intelligence in the workplace has prompted an unusually blunt call from a state-run newspaper to protect labor rights, as Beijing considers how to contain risks posed by the new technology.

item.title: Wizz Air Says Iran Conflict Cut into Earnings; Omits Outlook
item.description: Wizz Air Holdings Plc said the Iran conflict shaved €50 million from earnings in its fiscal full year, almost wiping out profit in the period, while vowing to take advantage of market dislocations to push for growth.

item.title: European Stocks Edge Higher Ahead of ECB Decision
Japan’s Shin-Etsu Plans New Rare-Earth Refinery to Secure Supply
item.description: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co., one of Japan’s largest rare-earth magnet makers, plans to build a new domestic refinery, the latest attempt by a company outside China to diversify its supply chain.

item.title: Albania’s Flamingo Revolution Spreads From Kushner Resort to Political Elite
item.description: As protests in Tirana extend into a second week, they have escalated from opposition to two €5 billion ($5.7 billion) planned resorts in ecologically sensitive areas linked to Jared Kushner into demands for the entire Albanian political establishment to step aside.
